steps.title: Convert g-values into f-values
steps.content:
Do not regress $g(x)$ directly, because the question asks for the $y$-intercept of $f$. Since $g(x)=\frac{f(x)}{x+3}$, multiply each $g(x)$ value by $x+3$:
  • $f(-27)=3(-24)=-72$
  • $f(-9)=0(-6)=0$
  • $f(21)=5(24)=120$

steps.title: Fit f in Desmos
steps.content: Now enter the converted points $(-27,-72)$, $(-9,0)$, and $(21,120)$ and run $y_1 \sim m x_1+b$. Desmos gives $m=4$ and $b=36$, so $f(x)=4x+36$.

steps.title: Answer the intercept question
steps.content: The $y$-intercept of $y=f(x)$ is $(0,b)$, so it is $(0,36)$. That matches choice A.
